description_long: This bicycle shop is a compact, open-front structure with two side walls and a back wall, featuring a large window with green shutters. The shop interior includes a wooden workbench, several stools, and a large bicycle mounted against the back wall, partially visible through the window. The left wall is adorned with a mirror, a radio, a towel, and a set of illustrated hairstyle options, suggesting a dual-purpose space, possibly also functioning as a barbershop. The floor is tiled in light gray, and the shop is decorated with a striped awning above the entrance. A prominent signboard with red Vietnamese text stands outside, indicating the shop's services. The overall atmosphere is utilitarian and inviting, with a blend of practical furnishings and local character.
description: A small, open-front bicycle shop with a tiled floor, green shutters, a mounted bicycle, and a signboard with Vietnamese text, featuring stools and a workbench inside.
description_view_0: Front-left view showing the open shop interior, bicycle mounted on the back wall, green shutters, stools, workbench, and the prominent signboard outside.
description_view_1: Direct front view highlighting the shop's entrance, central chair, wall-mounted mirror, illustrated hairstyle options, and the bicycle's side profile.
description_view_2: Rear view showing the plain exterior back wall with a window, through which the bicycle and some interior elements are partially visible.
description_view_3: Right-side exterior view displaying a blank wall with no windows or features visible.
attribute_bicycle_signage: A white signboard with red Vietnamese text ('Hớt Tóc') and smaller black text is placed outside the shop, facing the street. No pictograms or logos are visible.
attribute_display_of_bicycles: A single bicycle is displayed mounted against the back wall inside the shop, positioned horizontally and partially visible through the window.
